title: EnviTec Biogas Starts Biomethane Feed-in at New Cremona Plant
content: EnviTec Biogas has successfully commenced the feed-in of biomethane from its newly completed biogas and gas upgrading plant in Soncino, near Cremona, Italy. The facility, built for long-standing customer Dr. Francesco Avogadri, is now injecting 237 NmÂ³/h (250 SmÂ³/h) of biomethane into the public grid. Utilizing pig slurry and catch crops as feedstock, the plant features EnviTec's proprietary EnviThan three-stage membrane technology, which was manufactured in Germany. This project supports the customer's transition from fossil fuels to renewable energy. EnviTec S.r.l. currently has three additional Italian projects underway, including new constructions and expansions in Saerbeck, Grosseto, and Mantua. With the extension of Italy's National Recovery and Resilience Plan (PNRR), EnviTec is positioning itself to meet growing demand in the region's expanding biomethane market.
author: Analysts Account
